item.title: ASEAN's top diplomats meet in shadow of US-Iran conflict
item.description:
By Karen Lema

MANILA, July 21 (Reuters) - Tensions between the U.S. and Iran are likely to cast a shadow over a diplomatic gathering in Southeast Asia this week, injecting additional uncertainty into a region grappling with maritime tensions with China and a faltering regional effort to end Myanmar's civil war.

item.title: Israel says pilot safe zones begin in southern Lebanon
item.description:
July 20 (Reuters) - The Israeli military said on Monday it had begun a pilot programme for safe zones in southern Lebanon in cooperation with U.S. and Lebanese forces, in what a U.S. official called a step toward reasserting Lebanese state authority in the area.

The pilot zones are designated areas in southern Lebanon where a U.S.-brokered framework agreement between Israel and Lebanon signed last month foresees Iran-backed Hezbollah's disarmament, the withdrawal of Israeli forces and the deployment of Lebanese troops.

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, reinforces air assets in region: What to know
item.description: After weathering five months of airstrikes, the IRGC's strategy to deny the US Air Force safe harbor at runways across the region still shows lethal progress.

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, reinforces air assets in region: What to know
item.description: After weathering five months of airstrikes, the IRGC's strategy to deny the US Air Force safe harbor at runways across the region still shows lethal progress.

item.title: Netanyahu will not be arrested in US, Trump says, as New York mayor mulls options
item.description:
By Doina Chiacu

July 20 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested when he visits the U.S., after New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani said he was still considering the move.

"Benjamin Netanyahu will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form, while in the United States of America," Trump wrote in a social media post that did not refer to the mayor's comments.

item.title: US-Iran strikes: latest developments
item.description: Iran's president said his country is fighting a "full-scale war" with the United States, as Washington expanded its strikes across the Islamic republic and Tehran hit Gulf states. Iran in 'full-scale war' with US Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said his country was in a "full-scale war" with the United States and that Tehran should "accept the natural consequences of this resistance".

item.title: Iran's economy faces renewed pressure as ceasefire collapses
item.description: Tehran briefly regained billions in oil revenue under June's truce, raising questions about how long the Islamic Republic can withstand a fresh economic siege.

item.title: Iran's economy faces renewed pressure as ceasefire collapses
item.description: Tehran briefly regained billions in oil revenue under June's truce, raising questions about how long the Islamic Republic can withstand a fresh economic siege.

item.title: As Houthis declare Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, oil markets brace for impact
item.description: The Iran-backed group said the maritime embargo on the Gulf's biggest crude exporter would be effective immediately.

item.title: As Houthis declare Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, oil markets brace for impact
item.description: The Iran-backed group said the maritime embargo on the Gulf's biggest crude exporter would be effective immediately.

item.description: The Iran-backed group said the maritime embargo on the Gulf's biggest crude exporter would be effective immediately.

item.title: As Houthis declare Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, oil markets brace for impact

item.title: US attacks deepen hardship, not dissent, in Iran's restive south
item.description:
By Parisa Hafezi and Angus McDowall

DUBAI, July 20 (Reuters) - Opposition to Iran's clerical rulers has long simmered in the country's impoverished south. But after bearing the brunt of U.S. strikes this month, many residents are directing their anger at President Donald Trump and an air campaign they say has upended their lives.

item.title: Iran's Araghchi reveals Turkey's role in thwarting Kurdish offensive
item.description: The account offers Tehran's first public confirmation of reports that Ankara intervened to stop armed Iranian Kurdish groups from entering the conflict.

item.title: Iran's Araghchi reveals Turkey's role in thwarting Kurdish offensive
item.description: The account offers Tehran's first public confirmation of reports that Ankara intervened to stop armed Iranian Kurdish groups from entering the conflict.
